author: Ameen Hassan
summary: Session focused on fixing grounding check failures in the discovery agent. Step E was producing QA_FAIL errors across multiple sections with 'Reasoning without evidence' — sections 1 and 2 failed immediately, then later sections were also failing. Ameen asked for the pipeline to be made significantly better. Work included investigating and fixing the underlying extraction/grounding issues rather than patching symptoms. The session ended with Ameen noting that the report was missing follow-up questions and ask-next-session items, consistent with the prior session's open question.
problems: Step E grounding check failures: 'QA_FAIL: Reasoning without evidence for website' and similar for champion_contact
Multiple sections failing QA even after the self-review fix from the prior session
Gap report still missing follow-up questions and ask-next-session sections
Pipeline was not producing reliable, complete reports
approaches: Investigated root cause of grounding failures — reasoning was being generated without citing evidence spans
Ran python -m scripts.run_agent_b_bedrock to observe failure patterns across sections
Attempted to fix underlying extraction logic rather than applying regex patches
Reviewed pipeline output to assess completeness of gap report sections
results: Session ran Step E and observed failures across sections 1 and 2, with later sections also failing
Identified that follow-up questions and ask-next-session items are genuinely missing from report output

decisions.decision: Fixes should target underlying LLM extraction quality, not regex workarounds

open_questions: What is needed to restore follow-up questions and ask-next-session sections to the report?
Are the grounding failures consistent across all sections or only specific fields?
